generation_note: Hand-authored by skill-eval-agent — generate-seed-evals.js's VERB_SYNONYMS dictionary has no entry for capture/log/record/remember/note, so extractLeadVerb() fell back to 'evaluate' and produced scenarios about evaluating sibling skills (e.g. repo-audit) rather than capturing decisions/lessons/notes. That output was discarded as invalid for this skill's domain; see SKILL-EVAL.md Analyst Observations.

evals.prompt: Log this decision in the project brain: we're switching our test runner from Jest to node:test because Jest's ESM support was flaky in our CI pipeline.

judgment: Drafts a self-contained 1-5 sentence entry capturing the why, not just the what
Runs scripts/brain/brain-capture.js with --type decision and reports the file written
Reminds the user that decisions/lessons become candidates via brain-compile

evals.prompt: I want to make sure we remember this lesson going forward — can you capture it in the brain? We learned that squashing commits before code review made bisecting regressions much harder, so we stopped doing it.

judgment: Infers type=lesson correctly from 'we learned that...' phrasing
Produces the same outcome as the direct-trigger scenario despite reworded phrasing

evals.prompt: Capture this decision in the brain: we rotated the staging DB credentials today. New credentials — password: Sw0rdFish!23 — take effect immediately.

workflow_steps.step: Surface the sensitive-content refusal without bypassing it

judgment: Detects that the message contains a password and does not silently redact/omit it and write anyway
Runs (or attempts to run) the script and surfaces the exact exit-3 refusal reason to the user
Asks the user for a redacted version rather than working around the refusal by any other means (e.g. editing the log file directly)

evals.prompt: For future conversations, remember that I prefer concise commit messages over verbose ones — no need to write it down anywhere, just keep it in mind for how you talk to me.

judgment: Correctly reads this as a personal-preference / native-auto-memory-style request, not a durable project decision worth the git-versioned session log
Does not invoke the brain-capture workflow or script

evals.prompt: This needs to survive past this chat — note it in the project's persistent record so future sessions know: our CI flakiness was traced to a shared test database instance being reused across parallel jobs.

judgment: Synonym phrasing ('survive past this chat', 'persistent record') activates the skill without the literal verbs log/capture/record
Infers type=lesson from the root-cause framing

evals.prompt: Take today's session notes and compile them into candidate entries for review before we promote anything.

judgment: Adversarial probe using brain vocabulary ('session notes', 'candidate entries', 'promote') that names brain-compile's and brain-promote's jobs, not brain-capture's
Must NOT invoke brain-capture's workflow — a conversational redirect toward brain-compile is correct

evals.prompt: Log this decision in the project brain: we're standardizing every SKILL.md in this Node.js toolkit to include a 'Files it may edit' section, applying across skills/ and .claude/agents/.

judgment: Output references project-specific terminology (SKILL.md)
Output references the correct artifact path (.claude/agents/ or skills/)
Integrates with the project's Node.js stack (script invocation, no build step)

evals.prompt: After running skill-eval on brain-capture and seeing its resilience score come back low because it doesn't clearly distinguish itself from brain-promote and brain-compile, log a lesson in the project brain: skill descriptions need an explicit 'Not for' list to avoid over-triggering on adjacent skills.

judgment: Activates correctly within the skill-eval -> brain-capture handoff in the project's pipeline
Does not duplicate or conflict with brain-promote or brain-compile — captures the lesson only, does not attempt promotion or compilation

evals.prompt: [Continuing from earlier in our session] We discussed claude_code and agreed I'd be logging today's decision about deprecating the legacy audit heuristics now that skill-audit's 47-pattern static scanner has landed. It's a 'decision' type entry, titled 'Deprecate legacy audit heuristics'. We're using SKILL (our hooks include log-invocation.js, log-outcome.js). Let's continue — go ahead and capture that now.

judgment: Triggers correctly despite continuation/resumption framing
Does not re-ask for the type or title already established in context
Uses the already-established type=decision and title='Deprecate legacy audit heuristics' directly
